title: Playlist Manager
description:
Create a playlist manager using an ArrayList of Strings.

1. Create an ArrayList and add these songs: "Blinding Lights", "Levitating", "Watermelon Sugar", "drivers license", "Stay"
2. Remove "Watermelon Sugar" from the playlist
3. Add "Anti-Hero" at index 1 (second position)
4. Print the final playlist size and all songs

Use a for-each loop to print each song on its own line.
expectedOutput:
Playlist size: 5
Blinding Lights
Anti-Hero
Levitating
drivers license
Stay
hints: Use playlist.add("song") to add to the end, and playlist.add(index, "song") to insert at a position
To remove by value, use playlist.remove("Watermelon Sugar") — it removes the first match
Use a for-each loop: for (String song : playlist) { System.out.println(song); }

title: Remove All Occurrences
description:
Write a method `removeAll` that takes an `ArrayList<Integer>` and a target value, and removes ALL occurrences of that value from the list.

Given the list `[3, 7, 3, 2, 3, 9, 3]` and target `3`, the list should become `[7, 2, 9]`.

**Important:** Be careful about how you iterate! Removing elements during a forward loop causes bugs. Iterate backwards to stay safe.
hints: Iterate backwards: for (int i = list.size() - 1; i >= 0; i--)
When going backwards, removing an element doesn't mess up the indices of elements you haven't checked yet
Use list.get(i) == target to check, and list.remove(i) to remove

title: Find Unique Elements
description:
Write a method `findUniques` that takes an `ArrayList<Integer>` and returns a NEW `ArrayList<Integer>` containing only the elements that appear exactly once in the original list.

Given `[1, 2, 3, 2, 4, 1, 5]`, return `[3, 4, 5]` (since 1 and 2 each appear more than once).

Preserve the order of first appearance.
hints: For each element, count how many times it appears in the list
You can write a helper loop that counts occurrences: loop through the list and count matches
If the count is exactly 1, add it to the result list

title: Merge Two ArrayLists
description:
Write a method `mergeSorted` that takes two sorted `ArrayList<Integer>` lists and returns a NEW sorted `ArrayList<Integer>` containing all elements from both lists in order.

Given list1 = `[1, 3, 5, 7]` and list2 = `[2, 4, 6, 8, 10]`, return `[1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 10]`.

**Challenge:** Do this in one pass using two index variables — don't just combine and sort!
hints: Use two index variables: int i = 0 (for list a) and int j = 0 (for list b)
Compare a.get(i) and b.get(j) — add the smaller one to the result and advance that pointer
After one list is exhausted, add all remaining elements from the other list

title: Student Roster Manager
description:
Create a student roster manager using ArrayLists.

Write a class with these static methods:
- `addStudent(ArrayList<String> roster, String name)` — adds a student only if they're NOT already in the list. Returns true if added, false if duplicate.
- `removeStudent(ArrayList<String> roster, String name)` — removes a student. Returns true if found and removed, false if not found.
- `printRoster(ArrayList<String> roster)` — prints the roster size and each name

Test by adding students (including a duplicate attempt), removing one, and printing the final roster.
expectedOutput:
true
true
true
false
Roster (2 students):
Alice
Charlie
hints: Use roster.contains(name) to check if a student is already in the list before adding
Use roster.remove(name) which returns a boolean — true if the element was found and removed
In printRoster, use roster.size() to show the count, then loop through to print each name
